shorts.content: There are women holding entire worlds together before 9 AM, and still questioning if they've done enough. Writing something vulnerable isn't as scary as sharing it with the world.
shorts.title: Why sharing is scarier than writing

shorts.hook: Fear is a signal you're growing
shorts.content: Fear is often a signal that you're growing and moving in the right direction. Taking the leap requires moving forward even when your hands are shaking.
shorts.title: What fear really means for leaders

shorts.hook: Women hold worlds together by 9 AM
shorts.content: Women hold entire worlds together before 9 AM and still question if they've done enough. Invisible labor of women deserves recognition and honor in leadership conversations.
shorts.title: The invisible work women do every day

shorts.content: Leading with vulnerability and beginner's mind creates authentic connection. Perseverance through burnout and fear is part of the journey to authentic leadership.
shorts.title: The power of vulnerable leadership

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What happens when sharing becomes scarier than writing?
titleOptions.boldStatement: Writing My Book Wasn't the Scary Part (This Was)
titleOptions.numberBased: 3 Signs You're Ready to Share Your Most Vulnerable Work

description:
Sometimes the scariest part isn't creating something vulnerable—it's letting the world see it.

In this raw solo episode, I'm sharing why hitting 'publish' on my book was scarier than writing every vulnerable word.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 - Introduction: The real fear behind vulnerability
2:30 - Why writing wasn't the scary part
5:15 - Women holding worlds together before 9AM
8:45 - Leading with vulnerability and beginner's mind
12:20 - The invisible labor that deserves recognition
15:10 - Taking the leap with shaking hands
18:30 - Perseverance through burnout and fear

🔥 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Fear signals you're growing in the right direction
• Sharing vulnerability is harder than creating it
• Women's invisible labor deserves leadership recognition
• Authentic connection comes from leading with beginner's mind
• Moving forward despite fear is true courage

📖 Get the book: https://a.co/d/ej9A37z

📖 FULL SHOW NOTES: https://themanageher.com/blog/the-story-behind-aimee-rickabus-on-invisible-work-real-power-reclamation

📊 TAKE THE QUIZ: https://themanageher.com/blog/the-story-behind-aimee-rickabus-on-invisible-work-real-power-reclamation

🎙️ SUBSCRIBE to The Manage Her® Podcast for weekly leadership insights

#WomenLeadership #VulnerableLeadership #BookLaunch #AuthenticLeadership #WomenInBusiness #PersonalGrowth #Leadership #Empowerment #InvisibleLabor #CourageousLeadership

communityPosts.content:
Which feels scarier to you as a leader?

A) Creating something vulnerable and personal
B) Sharing that vulnerable work with the world

I just dropped a solo episode about why hitting 'publish' on my book was scarier than writing every raw, honest word. The results might surprise you! 

What's your experience been? Drop your thoughts below! 👇

"There are women holding entire worlds together before 9 AM, and still questioning if they've done enough."

This hit me hard while recording my latest solo episode. We carry so much invisible labor, yet still doubt our worth and contributions.

To every woman reading this: You ARE enough. Your unseen work matters. Your leadership matters.

What invisible work are you doing that deserves recognition? I see you. 💪✨

pinnedComment: The story behind this episode is just as vulnerable as the episode itself 📖 If this resonated with you, I dive deeper into fear, vulnerability, and the invisible work we do as women leaders on the blog. Plus there's a quiz to help you identify your leadership superpower! Check it out: https://themanageher.com/blog/the-story-behind-aimee-rickabus-on-invisible-work-real-power-reclamation What part of this episode hit you the most? 👇
